items.text:
Subject: Updated Details for the Geology Field Trip

Dear Students,

I am writing to confirm the new arrangements for our field trip to Granite Ridge State Park. Because of the storm last week, the trip has been moved to Saturday, October 18. We will meet outside Brennan Hall at 7:30 AM and return by 5:00 PM.

Please remember the following:
- The transportation fee of $15.00 is due at the Geology Department office by October 10.
- Bring sturdy boots, a water bottle, and a packed lunch, as no food will be sold at the site.
- Students who have not paid by the deadline cannot board the bus.

If you have any questions, please email me or stop by Room 214 during my office hours on Tuesday afternoons. I look forward to a productive day in the field.

Best regards,
Professor Lin

format_metadata.subject: Updated Details for the Geology Field Trip

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To advertise a new geology course offered next semester
options.B: To collect student feedback about an earlier field trip
options.C: To inform students of the rescheduled field trip details
options.D: To warn students about dangerous weather in the park

questions.explanation: The email opens by confirming the new arrangements for the field trip and then lists the updated date, meeting place, fee, and items to bring. Its overall purpose is to inform students of the rescheduled trip details, not to advertise a course, gather feedback, or issue a weather warning.

questions.stem: According to the email, what must students do by October 10?

options.A: To meet the group outside Brennan Hall at 7:30 AM
options.B: To pay the transportation fee at the department office
options.C: To bring sturdy boots and a packed lunch for the day
options.D: To email the professor during her Tuesday office hours

questions.explanation: The email states that the transportation fee of $15.00 is due at the Geology Department office by October 10. The meeting time, the items to bring, and contacting the professor are mentioned elsewhere but are not the action tied to the October 10 deadline.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who have not paid by the deadline?

options.A: They will receive a full refund of the fee later
options.B: They must bring extra food to share at the site
options.C: They can pay the driver in cash on the bus
options.D: They will not be permitted to join the field trip

questions.explanation: The email warns that students who have not paid by the deadline cannot board the bus, which implies they will not be able to join the trip. The other options either reverse this rule or add details the email never mentions.
